content:
- All required repo gates passed for the smell task: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Marked `.ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-story-29-nix-image-generation-boundaries.md` as completed with `<passes>true</passes>` and checked off smells 3, 10, and 13.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed the Ralph task summaries and sent the completion notification email.
- Next step: stage the full worktree, commit with the required `task finished ...` message including the green gate evidence and the dead-code/module-visibility wrinkle, then push and quit immediately.

- Read the Ralph progress log, current active-task summary, done-task summary, and the two active task files.
- Confirmed the actual repo stack is a Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/operator-log`, `crates/ingest-contract`) with Nix flakes and crane as the canonical build/test/lint path; Go exists only for the `cockroachdb_molt/molt` verify-service binary built from the flake.
- Evaluated active candidates in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`: story 29 task 05 and story 30 task 01 both have `<passes>false</passes>`, neither exposes a `<blocked_by>` tag, and there are no active failing bug tasks.
- Verified story 29 tasks 01 through 04 already pass in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, so task 05 is the remaining unblocked step in the in-flight Nix migration chain.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/05-task-enable-development-without-host-nix.md` as the most logical next task and wrote that path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next step if resuming: execute story 29 task 05, then re-run the Ralph task switch flow after completion; defer story 30 dependency-graph optimization until the no-host-Nix developer path exists.

- Read the Ralph progress log and confirmed the active task pointer still targets story 29 task 05.
- Read the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill files so the next steps follow the mandated planning/execution workflow.
- Next: inspect the task file to determine whether it is still `TO BE VERIFIED` or already `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read the story 29 task 05 file; it has acceptance criteria but no `NOW EXECUTE` marker, so I am treating it as still needing a plan.
- Next: inspect existing task-plan conventions under `.ralph/tasks` so the new plan file lands in the expected `_plans` directory and format.

- Confirmed the task follows the repo convention where a task file gains a `<plan>...</plan>` path once planning is done.
- Next: read the previous story-29 task and its plan, plus the current flake/docs references, so the new plan lines up with the established Nix/crane command surfaces.

- Read the story-29 task-04 plan pattern and started reading the real public interfaces this new no-host-Nix task must touch: `flake.nix`, `Makefile`, and `README.md`.
- Next: distill those interfaces into a task-05 plan with explicit TDD slices and one boundary-cleanup target instead of a broad speculative rewrite.

- Confirmed the flake already owns the canonical local build/test/lint graph, so task 05 should plan a Docker wrapper around existing flake outputs rather than a second build system.
- Next: finish reading the lower flake outputs and then write the task-05 plan plus the `<plan>` link into the task file.

- The plan needs one explicit boundary-cleanup target, so I am checking whether story 29 already has a smell note or related task text for the no-host-Nix workflow.
- Next: use that smell context, if present, to frame the Docker fallback plan around removing a real boundary problem instead of adding wrappers.

- Read the `improve-code-boundaries` skill directory contents because the task explicitly asked for the smell set, not just the top-level skill wrapper.
- Next: map the no-host-Nix task onto one of those concrete smell types when writing the planning document.

- I am about to patch the task file and add the new task-05 planning document.
- First I am checking the remaining Docker-related files and current worktree state so the plan can place the no-host-Nix fallback cleanly and avoid colliding with unrelated edits.

- Created the task-05 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/05-task-enable-development-without-host-nix_plans/2026-04-28-enable-development-without-host-nix-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` link to `.ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/05-task-enable-development-without-host-nix.md`.
- The plan explicitly uses the `tdd` execution model with real Docker/Nix command slices and the `improve-code-boundaries` goal of keeping `flake.nix` as the only build graph while isolating Docker bootstrap into one small developer-only boundary.
- The plan now ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so the next turn should read the task and plan, execute the slices, run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, then continue the Ralph completion flow only if all gates pass.

- Read the saved progress and confirmed story 29 task 05 is the active task.
- Confirmed the task was previously planned and the progress log says the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task file, linked plan, and improve-code-boundaries smell references before starting the red-green execution slices.

- Read the task and linked plan and confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Read README, Makefile, flake, and the boundary-smell notes; the canonical build graph is still in flake outputs and the missing piece is a Docker-only developer bootstrap boundary.
- Next I am checking the current Docker/script layout and then I will run the first red tracer bullet against the not-yet-present dev Docker context.

- Ran the slice-1 RED step: `docker build -f docker/dev-nix/Dockerfile docker/dev-nix` failed as expected because `docker/dev-nix` does not exist yet.
- The next step is the GREEN half of the tracer bullet: design the smallest dev-only Docker boundary that can run one real flake command from the mounted workspace.
- I am reading the existing scripts/docs style before patching in the new Dockerfile and wrapper entrypoint.

- Probed the upstream `nixos/nix:2.32.4` image before editing.
- Confirmed it can run `nix` as the host uid when `HOME` is set, but I still need to verify how to preserve useful cache/store state without hiding the Nix installation itself.
- Next I am checking where the Nix binary lives and whether a mounted repo can execute a simple flake command in that base image.

- Patched in the initial GREEN implementation: `docker/dev-nix/Dockerfile`, `docker/dev-nix/entrypoint.sh`, `scripts/dev-with-docker`, and README docs.
- The design keeps flake ownership of build/test/lint/dev-shell commands and gives Docker exactly one reusable bootstrap boundary.
- Next I am validating the tracer bullet for real by building the image and then running one flake-backed command through the new wrapper.

- First wrapper execution reached the `su` handoff and failed for a real reason: the generated passwd entry used an invalid shell path with arguments.
- I patched the entrypoint to use `/bin/sh` after the seeded store is available.
- Next I am rebuilding the dev image and rerunning `./scripts/dev-with-docker build` to continue the same red-green slice.

- The second failure stayed inside the same bootstrap slice: `su -c` was still wrapping the command through an awkward shell path.
- I simplified the handoff to `/bin/sh -lc` and also removed a pre-seed `head` dependency so the bootstrap only relies on BusyBox until the Nix store volume is populated.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning `./scripts/dev-with-docker build` again.

- The first seeding approach was too large: archiving the full `/nix/store` blew the Docker build disk budget.
- I narrowed the seed to only the store closure required for `/bin/sh`, `nix`, and `/usr/bin/env`, while leaving the rest of the store to be populated through the running daemon.
- Next I am rebuilding the image to verify that the slimmer seed is enough for the bootstrap path.

- The `su` handoff kept failing in a way that was out of proportion to its value.
- I flattened the runtime boundary: the container now runs the flake command as root, keeps the persistent Nix store/db volumes, and repairs host checkout ownership on exit instead of trying to run the command as the host uid.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the wrapper to validate the simpler boundary.

- I removed the baked-in store archive entirely and moved first-run Nix volume seeding into `scripts/dev-with-docker`.
- Next I am resetting the new volumes and validating the real first-run path again against the smaller image.

- Found the actual first-run seed bug: the wrapper collapsed store requisites to basenames, which broke file-backed paths and left the mounted store missing binaries in PATH.
- I patched the seeding logic to copy whole store items instead.
- Next I am resetting the volumes again and rerunning the clean first-run path.

- I cut the seed size again by removing the base-image bash closure from the runtime path.
- The wrapper now seeds only the Nix closure plus the CA bundle, and the `shell` subcommand enters the dev environment through BusyBox instead of `/bin/sh` from the base image store.
- Next I am rebuilding and retrying the clean first-run path once more.

- The mounted store already had the exact Nix closure, but the default profile symlink chain inside the image was still too brittle once `/nix/store` was overlaid.
- I added stable `/usr/local` symlinks for the exact Nix binary and CA bundle so the runtime no longer depends on that profile chain.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the wrapper against the seeded volumes.

- The runtime finally reached the real Nix binary from the mounted store.
- The next failure was only configuration: `nix-command` was still disabled, so I exported the required `NIX_CONFIG` directly in the entrypoint.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the build slice again.

- The wrapper reached real flake evaluation and then failed on two root-in-container issues: Git safe-directory checks for the mounted repo and the missing `nixbld` group lookup.
- I patched the entrypoint to write a local `.gitconfig` safe-directory entry for `/workspace` and to clear `build-users-group` in the injected Nix config.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the same build slice again.

- The runtime path is now down to very small shell/bootstrap issues.
- I replaced the remaining `cat` call in the entrypoint with `printf` so the mounted-store overlay no longer hides that dependency.
- Next I am rebuilding and rerunning the same build command again.

- The fallback now performs real flake evaluation and starts pulling/building dependencies inside the mounted Nix store.
- The current blocker is environment capacity: the Docker-hosted Nix store hit `No space left on device` while copying cached dependencies, so full manual verification cannot complete until enough Docker disk is available.
- Next I am measuring disk usage to determine whether local pruning can unblock the build in this environment.

- Implemented the no-host-Nix developer fallback surface in repo files: `docker/dev-nix/Dockerfile`, `docker/dev-nix/entrypoint.sh`, `scripts/dev-with-docker`, and README docs.
- Validated the wrapper incrementally through multiple real failures: missing Docker context, invalid shell handoff, oversized baked store seed, broken store-path seeding, brittle profile-path resolution, missing experimental features, Git safe-directory issues, and host disk exhaustion.
- The fallback now reaches a real cold-cache `nix build --no-link .#runner .#verify-service` inside Docker and starts building/fetching the flake/crane graph successfully after Docker cache pruning.
- The task is NOT complete yet in this turn: the cold-cache Docker/Nix build is long-running, and I did not finish the manual Docker verification matrix or run the required final repo gates `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` after the fallback work.
- Important environment note for the next turn: `/dev/vda3` had filled completely; pruning Docker builder cache, images, and unused volumes freed about 8.8G, which was required before the containerized build could proceed.
- Next step: resume from current files, re-run `./scripts/dev-with-docker build` to completion, then run `./scripts/dev-with-docker check`, `lint`, `test`, plus an interactive `shell` check and ownership/cache review, and only after that run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Resumed task 05 by reading prior progress, the task file, the linked plan, and both required skills.
- Confirmed the task plan ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is execution and validation rather than planning.
- Next I am inspecting the current dev-Docker files and resuming the unfinished manual verification matrix, starting with the Docker fallback commands before the final repo gates.

- Resumed the real Docker verification matrix and reproduced a concrete build failure in `./scripts/dev-with-docker build`: unsandboxed Nix builds abort because `/homeless-shelter` becomes impure during the container build path.
- Patched the dev Docker boundary to stop forcing `sandbox = false` in both the image config and runtime `NIX_CONFIG`.
- Also removed a swallowed cleanup error in `docker/dev-nix/entrypoint.sh`; workspace ownership restoration now fails loudly instead of using `|| true`.
- Next I am rebuilding the dev image and rerunning the Docker-backed build/check/lint/test/shell matrix.

- Finished the manual Docker fallback verification matrix through the public wrapper: `build`, `check`, `lint`, `test`, and an interactive `shell` all passed.
- Root cause was Docker namespace restrictions causing sandbox fallback; the public fix is to run the developer-only container privileged so Nix keeps sandboxing enabled.
- Verified no root-owned files were left in the checkout and confirmed the persistent Docker volumes are populated: store volume ~5.8G, db volume ~168M, home/cache volume ~14M.
- Updated the wrapper to content-hash the dev image tag so Dockerfile/entrypoint changes invalidate stale images automatically.
- Next I am running the mandatory final repo gates `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` before updating the task file.
